# WI-QUIC-CRT-0039 - QUIC client-side 0-RTT rejection cleanup work item

## Summary

Add the rejected-resumption cleanup branch that discards dormant ZeroRtt packet-protection material while leaving accepted attempts intact and keeping anti-replay support out of scope.

## Requirements Addressed

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0142

## Design Inputs

- ARC-QUIC-CRT-0037

## Planned Changes

- Discard dormant ZeroRtt packet-protection material when the PSK-capable branch is rejected at ServerHello.
- Keep the accepted branch intact so later admitted 0-RTT ownership still has the retained material it needs.
- Avoid introducing receive-side replay handling, key update, transfer, retry, or public early-data support in this cleanup slice.
- Add focused requirement-home coverage for rejected-branch cleanup and accepted-branch retention.

## Out of Scope

- 0-RTT receive handling
- server admission
- anti-replay enforcement
- key update
- transfer enablement
- retry enablement
- public API widening
- IsSupported widening
- broader stream-management parity
- opportunistic refactors unrelated to the rejected-resumption cleanup branch

## Verification Plan

Run focused requirement-home tests for the direct bridge-state cleanup helper, the runtime rejected-branch cleanup, the runtime accepted-branch retention case, the randomized cleanup boundary, and the public no-broad-support guard; run the permanent BenchmarkDotNet suite for the rejected-vs-accepted cleanup branch; then run SpecTrace render and validation checks.

## Completion Notes

The managed client/runtime path now discards dormant ZeroRtt packet-protection material when the PSK-capable branch is rejected at ServerHello, keeps the accepted branch intact, and leaves receive-side replay controls, key update, transfer, retry, and public API widening out of scope.
